evidence: 10 post-v4 files (Apr 19, 35 total entries). nestjs-nest: 4/13 still use banned generic templates ('What production-readiness problems exist in...'), 1 empty quality-gate failure entry. axios-axios: 3/10 generic templates. tokio-rs-tokio: 2/6 generic. Code gate regression: output_detail dropped from 0.90 to 0.72 — v4 removed the good/bad CODE EXAMPLE GATE examples that v3 had, and the model stopped following the gate as strictly (axios shows only 5/10 entries with code blocks). v4's 'ban list' approach failed: the model ignores prohibitions but complied when shown examples. Generic template ratio is ~35% of total entries.
v5_hypothesis: v4's 'ban list' approach failed — the model ignores prohibitions but follows example patterns. v5 adds 5 explicit TRANSFORMATION EXAMPLES showing generic-to-specific rewrites, and restores the CODE EXAMPLE GATE good/bad examples that v4 removed. Targeting both instruction_clarity (0.62) and output_detail regression (0.72).
